title: Children Of A Lesser Clod

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes in his car during opening credits
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of pain/frustration
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - iconic character moment but routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: [Laughs] I know. We'll never see any of you again.
jokes.setup: YMCA offers free trial classes for potential members
jokes.punchline: Employee admits the obvious truth that free samples rarely convert to paying customers
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about business realities - well-crafted honesty

jokes.text: You got that right.
jokes.setup: YMCA employee's cynical admission about free trials
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately agrees he won't pay, confirming the employee's point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely lacks self-awareness about his cheapness

jokes.text: Well, it's your turn to get the ball out of the peach basket.
jokes.setup: Basketball game is described as 'high-scoring'
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're playing primitive basketball with actual peach baskets
jokes.explanation: Clever historical reference to basketball's origins - good visual gag setup

jokes.text: I'll get a ball out of your peach basket one of these days.
jokes.setup: Complaint about retrieving ball from peach basket
jokes.punchline: Vaguely threatening sexual innuendo using 'peach basket'
jokes.explanation: Weak sexual innuendo - forced and not particularly clever

jokes.text: I came to this country in '83 by cartwheeling over Berlin Wall.
jokes.setup: Romanian gymnastics coach introduction
jokes.punchline: Absurd method of escape using gymnastics skills
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific absurdist humor - perfectly integrates his gymnastics expertise with Cold War history

jokes.text: No giggling! It weakens the haunches!
jokes.setup: Girl giggles in gymnastics class
jokes.punchline: Bizarre anatomical claim about giggling affecting leg muscles
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - absurd but delivered with coach's serious authority

jokes.text: Eight? Too old! Go home, Grandma!
jokes.setup: Coach asks young girl's age
jokes.punchline: Calls 8-year-old 'too old' and 'Grandma'
jokes.explanation: Escalates the gymnastics age pressure to absurd extreme - good character consistency

jokes.text: Lugash must go next door to anger management class.
jokes.setup: Coach shows aggressive behavior toward child
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's taking anger management - highlighting the irony
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for the next joke - shows he clearly needs the class

jokes.text: Worthless anger management class! I hate it so much! I spit on it!
jokes.setup: Coach going to anger management class
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates uncontrolled anger about the anger management class
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff - character defeats the purpose of anger management while going to it

jokes.text: Yo, boy, this class is tight. You go from 'sloppa' to 'proppa.'
jokes.setup: Etiquette class setting established
jokes.punchline: Teacher uses street slang to promote proper behavior
jokes.explanation: Good contrast humor - street language promoting refinement

jokes.text: But the black man said- / Are you accusing my husband of misleading you? Good gracious!
jokes.setup: Bart references what the hip-hop teacher said
jokes.punchline: Proper lady escalates to defend husband's honor in overly formal way
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Bart's directness and lady's propriety

jokes.text: I should bust a cap in your ass!
jokes.setup: Proper etiquette lady defending husband's honor
jokes.punchline: Uses violent street slang despite being in etiquette class
jokes.explanation: Excellent character contradiction - proper lady using hardcore street threats

jokes.text: Pick me! Pick me! I got hoop dreams, Coach! I got 'em bad!
jokes.setup: Basketball team selection
jokes.punchline: Homer desperately wants to be picked, references 'Hoop Dreams' documentary
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike enthusiasm is character-appropriate but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Losers! Losers, losers, l- / You said Homer, right? / Losers!
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating being picked for team
jokes.punchline: Calls others losers, double-checks he was really picked, continues insulting
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Homer's insecurity masked by false bravado

jokes.text: What with the passing, and the dribbling... and my shoes made of the flubber! And away I go! It's frightening.
jokes.setup: Professor Frink gets picked for basketball team
jokes.punchline: His scientific explanation of basketball includes fictional 'flubber' shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frink character moment - mixing science terminology with pop culture reference

jokes.text: Ow, that was painful. Oh, the flubber is burning my feet!
jokes.setup: Frink's flubber shoes reference
jokes.punchline: Immediate consequences of his fictional technology
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback showing consequences of Frink's inventions

jokes.text: I plan to tear it down and turn the land into a nature preserve... where I will hunt the deadliest game of all- Man!
jokes.setup: Wolfcastle announces purchasing YMCA to make nature preserve
jokes.punchline: Reveals sinister plan to hunt humans
jokes.explanation: Good dark turn on nature preserve concept - references 'Most Dangerous Game' story

jokes.text: I am taking the ball and going home. / Hey! Hey, that's not your ball! / [Rifle Clicks] The chase begins.
jokes.setup: Carl takes ball and leaves like a child
jokes.punchline: Wolfcastle begins hunting him with a rifle
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from childish behavior to life-threatening situation

jokes.text: Boy, I am so sick of everyone assuming I'm good at basketball because I'm African-American!
jokes.setup: Carl making impressive basketball moves
jokes.punchline: Complains about racial stereotyping while demonstrating the stereotype
jokes.explanation: Social commentary humor with good ironic timing

jokes.text: Go, Carl. Go, Carl. It's my birthday. It's my birthday. Three-peat! You got mail, baby!
jokes.setup: Carl playing basketball well despite his complaints
jokes.punchline: String of random sports/celebration clichés
jokes.explanation: Scattered sports references without much focus - feels more random than crafted

jokes.text: Willie tickles the twine for two! Oy! Oy!
jokes.setup: Willie makes a basketball shot
jokes.punchline: Uses sports announcer language with his Scottish expressions
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific celebration combining basketball terminology with Willie's mannerisms

jokes.text: No way am I taking Groundskeeper Willie.
jokes.setup: Team strategy discussion for basketball coverage
jokes.punchline: Homer refuses to guard Willie for unexplained reasons
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment but no clear reason for the refusal makes it less funny

jokes.text: [Grunts] Eat my dust, mortals!
jokes.setup: Willie playing basketball aggressively
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top dramatic language for casual game
jokes.explanation: Willie's intense personality applied to sports creates good character humor

jokes.text: Oh, my goodness, an untied shoelace. I'll just get down on one knee and re-tie that. [Grunts] Eat my dust, mortals!
jokes.setup: Homer notices untied shoelace during game
jokes.punchline: Uses it as excuse to crawl/run in underhanded basketball move
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment of fake politeness masking cheating behavior

jokes.text: Homer, I'm afraid you've torn out your anterior cruciate ligament. / Did you say anterior? / [Screaming]
jokes.setup: Doctor explains Homer's knee injury
jokes.punchline: Homer panics at 'anterior' thinking it means 'interior'
jokes.explanation: Good Homer ignorance moment - medical terms confusing him

jokes.text: Maybe a little morphine would refresh my memory.
jokes.setup: Doctor asks how injury happened
jokes.punchline: Homer suggests drugs would help him remember
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer drug-seeking behavior disguised as medical necessity

jokes.text: I don't know. I'm still a little hazy. / Now I remember it like it was yesterday. / It happened today. / Hey, man, you're harshing my buzz.
jokes.setup: Homer claims haziness after getting morphine
jokes.punchline: Doctor corrects that injury was today, Homer uses drug slang
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's drug-affected state and inappropriate response

jokes.text: Ow! Jetson!
jokes.setup: Homer gets injured during flashback
jokes.punchline: Random exclamation of cartoon character name instead of curse
jokes.explanation: Random but character-appropriate substitute curse - Homer's pop culture mind

jokes.text: Maybe a little morphine would get me there quicker.
jokes.setup: Doctor says they need to get Homer to surgery
jokes.punchline: Homer again suggests morphine as solution to different problem
jokes.explanation: Running gag of Homer's drug-seeking becoming repetitive

jokes.text: One more and I get a free hysterectomy.
jokes.setup: Lisa notes how often Homer has surgery
jokes.punchline: References frequent customer rewards for inappropriate medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor combining frequent customer cards with medical absurdity - very quotable

jokes.text: What spinal cord injury? / Oh, he fell off the gurney.
jokes.setup: Doctor mentions spinal cord injury Homer didn't know about
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of additional injury from hospital incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of medical mishaps with casual delivery

jokes.text: Miss work? But my life would be nothing without the nucleon plant.
jokes.setup: Doctor says Homer will miss work during recovery
jokes.punchline: Homer pretends to care about job he notoriously hates
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - obvious insincerity about work dedication

jokes.text: You shouldn't have ordered all those hospital haircuts and porno films. / But Doctor Screwlittle sounded like a delightful romp.
jokes.setup: Homer's hospital bill includes inappropriate charges
jokes.punchline: Homer defends porn choice based on punny medical-sounding title
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - choosing inappropriate content based on innocent-sounding title

jokes.text: We've never welshed on a- Look, a bear! Run!
jokes.setup: Marge assuring they pay their bills
jokes.punchline: Abandons moral statement to flee from bear
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from moral high ground to survival instinct

jokes.text: Two weeks? What am I supposed to do? Just sit on my ass and watch TV? That ain't my style, man.
jokes.setup: Doctor tells Homer to rest for two weeks
jokes.punchline: Homer rejects lifestyle that is exactly his normal routine
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic character moment - Homer rejecting his actual lifestyle as foreign

jokes.text: No, they don't.
jokes.setup: Doctor says people in next room have it worse than Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer flatly denies this after looking
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-centered perspective delivered with perfect deadpan

jokes.text: Boingy, boingy, boingy, boingy, boingy.
jokes.setup: Barney approaching Homer's house
jokes.punchline: Sound effects suggest he's bouncing/stumbling drunkenly
jokes.explanation: Typical Barney drunk behavior - character appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Would you make me some Jell-O?
jokes.setup: Homer can't play with Barney
jokes.punchline: Makes pathetic request for comfort food like sick child
jokes.explanation: Homer's pathetic invalid behavior - character appropriate

jokes.text: So, the tables have turned! Now you're the shut-in, and I'm the hip young dude!
jokes.setup: Ned visiting injured Homer
jokes.punchline: Ned claims to be hip and young while gloating
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal moment - Ned's rare moment of superiority

jokes.text: There goes my wagging finger.
jokes.setup: Ned boasting about being the hip young dude
jokes.punchline: Immediately injures himself, showing his frailty
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Ned's brief moment of superiority - excellent timing

jokes.text: Hitch up your pants, air whittle.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting activities for bored Homer
jokes.punchline: Lists stereotypical old man activities
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about elderly stereotypes

jokes.text: Uh, Mr. Simpson, you weren't supposed to leave the home. / Thank you, Ping-Pong. / My name is Craig. / Sure, it is.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently wandering from retirement home
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual racism in refusing to believe Asian man's American name
jokes.explanation: Homer's ignorance played for comedy but relies on racial stereotyping

jokes.text: I know. I'll breed the pets... to each other. [Evil Laugh]
jokes.setup: Homer looking for something to do while injured
jokes.punchline: Decides to breed cat and dog together with mad scientist laugh
jokes.explanation: Good absurd Homer scheme with appropriate mad scientist energy

jokes.text: Soon I will have a miracle hybrid... with the loyalty of a cat and the cleanliness of a dog!
jokes.setup: Homer's pet breeding scheme
jokes.punchline: Gets the stereotypical traits backwards (cats are clean, dogs loyal)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ignorance - confidently wrong about basic animal traits

jokes.text: No. That's not how you do it!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to make pets breed
jokes.punchline: Gives pets advice on how to mate
jokes.explanation: Continuation of absurd breeding scheme with Homer as incompetent instructor

jokes.text: Oh, it feels so good to talk to another human being!
jokes.setup: Homer alone trying to breed pets
jokes.punchline: Desperate relief at human contact after failed animal project
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's isolation and social needs

jokes.text: Stupid Flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing relief at human contact
jokes.punchline: Immediately remembering it's Ned and expressing annoyance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer-Ned dynamic with perfect character consistency

jokes.text: [Laughs] It's gonna be one wholesome evening.
jokes.setup: Ned mentions Christian rock concert
jokes.punchline: His earnest excitement about 'wholesome' entertainment
jokes.explanation: Standard Ned character moment - his innocent enthusiasm for clean entertainment

jokes.text: That's not my Uncle Lou. And this man's not dead. / That's what I've been trying to tell you! / That's just gas escaping.
jokes.setup: Marge identifying body at morgue
jokes.punchline: Body isn't dead, then 'dies' again mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about morgue incompetence with escalating reveals

jokes.text: And this pickle you're offering only sweetens the deal.
jokes.setup: Ned asks for babysitting favor, mentions being 'in a pickle'
jokes.punchline: Homer takes idiom literally and values the actual pickle
jokes.explanation: Good Homer literalism - missing idiom meaning for food

jokes.text: Daddy's not here, is he? [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Kids say daddy told them to be polite to adults
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately encourages rule-breaking in parent's absence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer bad influence moment - undermining parental authority

jokes.text: Pokémon? Pokémon? With the pokey and the man... and the- [Gibberish]
jokes.setup: Kids mention wanting to play Pokémon
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused attempt to understand modern kid culture
jokes.explanation: Good generational gap humor - Homer's complete bewilderment at kid trends

jokes.text: He told us how the world keeps screwing him over.
jokes.setup: Kids reporting on fun time with Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer shared his adult cynicism with innocent children
jokes.explanation: Good inappropriate sharing - Homer treating kids like therapy session

jokes.text: Well, sir, I've never heard a preacher use the M-F word so many times.
jokes.setup: Homer asks about the 'wholesome' Christian rock concert
jokes.punchline: Ned's shocked innocence about profanity at religious event
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Ned's expectations about Christian entertainment

jokes.text: Don't tell me what I'm not doing. / So you are running a day care center? / Oh, you'd like that, wouldn't you?
jokes.setup: Ned says Homer's not running a day care
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive responses that commit him to doing it
jokes.explanation: Good Homer stubbornness leading to unintended commitment

jokes.text: Then I'll do it, just to see the look on your face. / That's the one.
jokes.setup: Homer commits to day care to spite Ned
jokes.punchline: Ned's pleased reaction defeats Homer's purpose
jokes.explanation: Excellent character interaction - Homer's spite backfiring perfectly

jokes.text: Why did you put my name on the sign? / It's just a legal thing to protect me.
jokes.setup: Day care sign shows Marge's name
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission of liability avoidance
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual dishonesty and self-interest

jokes.text: It's healing over my hand.
jokes.setup: Kid touching Homer's gross knee wound
jokes.punchline: Wound apparently healing around the child's hand
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with absurd healing properties

jokes.text: It knows you're afraid.
jokes.setup: Kid's hand being 'healed over' by Homer's wound
jokes.punchline: Homer treats his wound like a predatory entity
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd escalation - wound as sentient predator

jokes.text: I would like to take advantage of your baby prison. / We're calling it day care. / Yes, whatever. Just take them!
jokes.setup: Parent dropping off kids at Homer's day care
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'baby prison' and desperately wants to abandon children
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parental desperation - 'baby prison' is very quotable

jokes.text: We're calling it day care.
jokes.setup: Parent calling it 'baby prison'
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact correction to euphemistic term
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to parent's dark terminology

jokes.text: Any medical things I should know about? / Yes, probably!
jokes.setup: Marge asks about children's medical needs
jokes.punchline: Parent's casual admission of unknown health issues
jokes.explanation: Good negligent parent humor with casual delivery

jokes.text: I owe it all to my rewarding work with children. And not picking at it.
jokes.setup: Doctor notes Homer's wound has healed well
jokes.punchline: Homer credits noble work and basic hygiene equally
jokes.explanation: Homer equating noble sentiment with basic behavior

jokes.text: Being with those kids gives me a high only morphine can top. / You got any?
jokes.setup: Homer describing joy from working with children
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for drugs after making comparison
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's drug-seeking while undermining his noble sentiment

jokes.text: Dad's been doing that for two hours! / I've never seen the baby get tired of it before the adult.
jokes.setup: Homer playing peekaboo with baby
jokes.punchline: Role reversal - adult outlasting baby's attention span
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about adult/child role expectations

jokes.text: I can't remember the last time Dad sang us a torch song.
jokes.setup: Homer singing melancholy song to other kids
jokes.punchline: Lisa's specific musical knowledge noting the genre
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment - her musical sophistication and longing

jokes.text: You'll have children of your own someday. Then you'll know my joy.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa wondering why Homer ignores them for other kids
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses his actual children while prioritizing others
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental blindness - Homer not recognizing his own children's needs

jokes.text: You haven't seen the real Homer. It's all burping and neglect. / I think we know your dad a little bit better than you do, Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to warn kids about Homer's true nature
jokes.punchline: Kids dismiss Bart's expertise about his own father
jokes.explanation: Good ironic situation - outsiders claiming to know parent better than child

jokes.text: Boobs! Heinie! Mitten!
jokes.setup: Homer's song encouraging swearing
jokes.punchline: Kids' innocent/mild 'swears' including random non-swear word
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing kids' innocent interpretation of 'swearing'

jokes.text: If you're happy and you know it, say a swear / Boobs! / Heinie! / Mitten!
jokes.setup: Homer's version of children's song encouraging swearing
jokes.punchline: Kids' attempts at 'swearing' include innocent word 'mitten'
jokes.explanation: Good corruption of innocent children's song with escalating inappropriateness

jokes.text: They're always laughing. Laughing at nothing.
jokes.setup: Kids having fun with Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart's jealous observation about other kids' happiness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's jealousy and family dynamics

jokes.text: How come parents are always nicer to other kids than they are to their own? / I guess Dad just takes us for granted.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa watching Homer favor other children
jokes.punchline: Universal truth about parental behavior with others' kids
jokes.explanation: Excellent universal observation about family dynamics - very relatable

jokes.text: This'll give his heartstrings a much-needed plucking.
jokes.setup: Lisa planning revenge on Homer
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated metaphor for emotional manipulation from 8-year-old
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character voice with sophisticated language for child's scheme

jokes.text: That was 'tree-mendous!' [Chuckles] Where's Ralph?
jokes.setup: Homer making tree-related pun after activity
jokes.punchline: Casually notices child is missing after dangerous activity
jokes.explanation: Good negligent caregiver humor with Homer's casual attitude to danger

jokes.text: I almost died!
jokes.setup: Homer asking where Ralph is
jokes.punchline: Ralph's matter-of-fact report of near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph delivery - innocent report of serious danger

jokes.text: I contributed by not objecting too much.
jokes.setup: Award presenter mentions Homer's day care work
jokes.punchline: Marge's minimal contribution claim with typical honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge honesty about her minimal role in Homer's scheme

jokes.text: I contributed by not objecting too much.
jokes.setup: Awards ceremony praising Homer's community work
jokes.punchline: Marge's minimal-effort contribution claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge deadpan delivery about her passive support role

jokes.text: Don't film this!
jokes.setup: Film crew setting up to document Homer's award-worthy work
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately has something he doesn't want filmed
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's public vs private behavior contradiction

jokes.text: We're just resting our eyes.
jokes.setup: Bart complains about teamsters sleeping in his room
jokes.punchline: Classic euphemism for sleeping on the job
jokes.explanation: Standard union worker joke - predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Ow! You stepped on my arm! / Sorry. Daddy thought you were a pile of cable.
jokes.setup: Bart getting hurt in his own room
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual explanation for stepping on his son
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual neglect of his own child while focusing on filming

jokes.text: Yes. Through the magic of caring.
jokes.setup: Interviewer asks if Homer made the heart decorations himself
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-profound response about simple craft work
jokes.explanation: Homer's pretentious explanation for basic arts and crafts

jokes.text: Well, I've been cutting the felt kind of crooked.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about getting back at Dad
jokes.punchline: Homer's mundane confession about craft quality
jokes.explanation: Homer missing the point of Lisa's revenge plotting

jokes.text: Stop the music! Stop it! One more line, and we have to pay for the song!
jokes.setup: Award show opening with song performance
jokes.punchline: Host breaks fourth wall about music licensing costs
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about entertainment industry economics

jokes.text: Oh, right. The bus disaster-very sad.
jokes.setup: Host making small talk
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of major tragedy as small talk
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about media's casual treatment of tragedy

jokes.text: Oh, right. The bus disaster-very sad.
jokes.setup: Awards ceremony attempting to be topical
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of tragic news event
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about media's superficial treatment of tragedy

jokes.text: You can walk all over these doormats. [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Introducing award for 'people pleasers'
jokes.punchline: Calls nominees 'doormats' - literal and figurative meaning
jokes.explanation: Basic pun without much cleverness - standard award show humor

jokes.text: You can walk all over these doormats.
jokes.setup: Awards for 'biggest people pleaser'
jokes.punchline: Host insulting the nominees he's supposed to honor
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited humor that fits bad awards show character

jokes.text: So all of you out there, type 'control backslash semicolon alt dot escape'... and you'll be fine.
jokes.setup: Host mentions technical glitch in webcast
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous keyboard combination as 'fix' for technical problem
jokes.explanation: Good early internet era humor about nonsensical tech solutions

jokes.text: If you can find a sturdier hypno-coin, you buy it.
jokes.setup: Thanking sponsor Hanson's Hypno-Coins
jokes.punchline: Absurd product endorsement for fictional hypnosis device
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist product parody with quality guarantee format

jokes.text: would make Santa Claus himself vomit with rage.
jokes.setup: Host describing worthiness of award winner
jokes.punchline: Extreme image of Santa's violent nausea from not giving award
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd escalation - combining Christmas icon with violent imagery

jokes.text: Who writes this stuff?
jokes.setup: Host's absurd Santa vomiting line
jokes.punchline: Host's genuine confusion about his own material
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about bad writing with character breaking fourth wall

jokes.text: Who writes this stuff?
jokes.setup: Host delivering terrible scripted material
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to complain about writers
jokes.explanation: Standard meta-humor about bad writing - breaking fourth wall

jokes.text: Please let me win this Lord, 'cause if you don't-
jokes.setup: Homer praying to win award
jokes.punchline: About to threaten God if he doesn't win
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - treating God like a transaction partner

jokes.text: All my love has come back in trophy form.
jokes.setup: Homer winning the good guy award
jokes.punchline: Reduces love and recognition to physical trophy
jokes.explanation: Homer's materialistic view of emotional validation

jokes.text: His scabby red knee became an infectious beacon of hope.
jokes.setup: Documentary praising Homer's day care work
jokes.punchline: Describing gross wound as inspirational with 'infectious' double meaning
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay on 'infectious' - medical and inspirational meanings

jokes.text: Big phony!
jokes.setup: Documentary praising Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart's simple, direct contradiction interrupting ceremony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - cutting through ceremony with blunt truth

jokes.text: A beer-drinking meanie!
jokes.setup: Bart exposing Homer's true nature
jokes.punchline: Childish but accurate description of Homer
jokes.explanation: Good Bart language - kid-appropriate insults for adult behavior

jokes.text: Oh! I almost had a straight!
jokes.setup: Video evidence of Homer gambling
jokes.punchline: Homer's focus on his poker hand rather than being exposed
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced priorities - caring about game while reputation crumbles

jokes.text: We just spliced in some home movies. What do you think?
jokes.setup: Award show audience confused by video evidence
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual admission of sabotaging the ceremony
jokes.explanation: Good Bart mischief - matter-of-fact about elaborate revenge scheme

jokes.text: I'll mace you good!
jokes.setup: Homer being exposed at award ceremony
jokes.punchline: Threatens violence with non-lethal weapon
jokes.explanation: Homer's escalation to violence when exposed - mildly threatening but not lethal

jokes.text: My polite indignation knows no bounds!
jokes.setup: Crowd turning against Homer
jokes.punchline: Ned's contradiction - claiming boundless polite anger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - oxymoronic polite outrage

jokes.text: Come back here with my young'un!
jokes.setup: Homer fleeing with children
jokes.punchline: Cletus's hillbilly dialect for 'child'
jokes.explanation: Standard Cletus dialect humor - character appropriate but predictable

jokes.text: Uh, for frosty chocolate milk shakes! [Insane Laugh]
jokes.setup: Kids asking where Homer is taking them
jokes.punchline: Obviously false promise with maniacal laugh
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent lie to children during kidnapping situation

jokes.text: I'm gonna try to nail the driver with one of my shoes. / Arnie, please leave this to the police. / I'm sick of being a reporter. I want to make the news!
jokes.setup: News helicopter covering chase
jokes.punchline: Reporter wanting to participate rather than observe
jokes.explanation: Good media satire - reporter's ethics breaking down during exciting story

jokes.text: You're not the time, Kent! You're not the time!
jokes.setup: Kent telling Arnie 'this is not the time'
jokes.punchline: Arnie's nonsensical retort turning phrase back on Kent
jokes.explanation: Good nonsensical comeback from unhinged reporter

jokes.text: Then you better turn.
jokes.setup: Homer says they're not going to jail
jokes.punchline: Ralph's practical observation about their direction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - innocent logic cutting through adult drama

jokes.text: Now he's realizing he's too fat. / He's digging a hole like a dog. / Now he's given up on that and he's running back and forth.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to escape over fence
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of increasingly pathetic escape attempts
jokes.explanation: Good escalating physical comedy description - Homer's desperation

jokes.text: His legs are dangling in a comical fashion. / Oh, it's the saddest thing I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Homer stuck in pipe during escape
jokes.punchline: Contradiction between 'comical' and 'saddest' descriptions
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction in reporter's tone - comedy and pathos mixed

jokes.text: I can't see through metal, Kent!
jokes.setup: Kent asking about children's condition in vehicle
jokes.punchline: Arnie's obvious statement about limitations of vision
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to unrealistic media expectations

jokes.text: Was it because I showered love on those other children while ignoring you? / Yep. / Pretty much.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why kids exposed him
jokes.punchline: Kids' matter-of-fact confirmation of his accurate self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good resolution - Homer's rare moment of self-awareness confirmed by kids

jokes.text: The teamsters said they'd pick it up by 5:00. / Yeah. That'll happen.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions teamsters picking up snack table
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sarcastic response about union reliability
jokes.explanation: Standard union work ethic joke - callback to earlier teamster appearance

jokes.text: Pokémon? Pokémon? With the pokey and the man... and the- [Gibberish]
jokes.setup: End credits scene referencing earlier Pokémon confusion
jokes.punchline: Bill Cosby impression doing Homer's confused Pokémon bit
jokes.explanation: Weak end credits callback - dated celebrity impression without much added humor

tmdb_overview: After spraining his knee during a basketball game, Homer begins taking care of the neighborhood kids to cure his boredom, prompting jealousy from Bart and Lisa, who feel that Homer is giving the kids the attention they never had.
